Why Sony Interactive Entertainment?

Sony Interactive Entertainment isn’t just the Best Place to Play — it’s also the Best Place to Work. Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is the company behind the PlayStation brand. As a subsidiary of Sony Group Corporation, we’re part of a proud legacy of innovation and excellence. SIE is a dynamic technology company, delivering cutting-edge hardware and network services to more than 100 million people and an entertainment leader, home to some of the most beloved and recognizable intellectual properties (IP) in the world. Our role at SIE is to create and nurture the experiences under the PlayStation brand, a name synonymous with entertainment excellence and creativity.

Software Development Engineer In Test, ML/AI

PlayStation offers more than just the Best Place to Play; it is also a top workplace. Today, we are a global entertainment leader. Our portfolio includes PlayStation®5, PlayStation®4, PlayStation®VR, PlayStation®Plus, and well-known software from PlayStation Studios.

PlayStation works to foster an inclusive environment where employees feel supported and diversity is valued. We encourage individuals with passion and curiosity for innovation, technology, and play to apply for our open roles and become part of our growing distributed team.

The PlayStation brand falls under Sony Interactive Entertainment, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Sony Group Corporation.

At SIE, we believe in making play safer for everyone. Within our Information Security team, we protect our people, platforms, and products with smart, scalable, and thoughtful solutions. Our mission is to provide clear, actionable risk and security insights that guide the business and enable amazing player experiences.

We’re building a team that thrives on collaboration, curiosity, and delivering real value. Come help us make a difference!

We are hiring a Software Engineer in Test specializing in ML/AI quality, including automation for model evaluation, LLM-assisted test generation, and validation of AI-powered workflows. The role includes guiding quality strategy and developing automation frameworks. You will also manage implementation for complex, cross-functional, machine learning-powered products and services. This role involves more than test development. You will lead quality initiatives from start to finish, ensuring teams stay synchronized, dependencies are managed, and releases are delivered confidently. You will work closely with ML, engineering, product, and inf

  • Solid understanding of multithreading, concurrency, and low-level programming concepts.

06450017b05a.title: Semiconductor Characterization Lab Engineer
06450017b05a.description: We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled professional to join our electrical characterization lab. As a Lab Engineer, you will oversee the daily operations, safety protocols, and tool uptime of a high-voltage laboratory. At the same time, you will perform various electrical measurements on cutting-edge silicon carbide semiconductor devices. You will help bridge the gap between physical lab operations and high-precision electrical engineering by commissioning, troubleshooting, and repairing test benches and related hardware. You will also support the development of specialized test benches for electrical testing of high-voltage semiconductors. You will also be responsible for lab logistics, inventory management, and coordination of equipment calibration. This role offers the opportunity to work with an international team of engineers and technicians across multiple Bosch locations worldwide. Responsibilities: Perform electrical characterization measurements on silicon carbide semiconductor devices. Act as the primary safety responsible for the high-voltage testing environment. Support the bring-up, debugging, and troubleshooting of test infrastructure for high-voltage and high-current testing of semiconductor devices. Manage the lab equipment calibration schedule to minimize downtime and ensure high accuracy. Collaborate with the engineering team on the development and procurement of advanced testing solutions to support evolving lab requirements. Perform soldering, PCB rework, repairs, and component replacement as needed. Coordinate sample build-up logistics, including shipping, communication with external sample shops, and traceability. Support the team’s continuous improvement activities. Degree in Electrical Engineering, Electronics Engineering, or a related technical discipline. 2+ years of hands-on experience in an electronics or power engineering laboratory environment. Strong understanding of electrical engineering fundamentals. Experience in using common lab equipment such as oscilloscopes, power supplies, DMMs, function generators, etc. Ability to read and understand schematics/datasheets. Strong communication skills, a team-oriented mindset, and the ability to collaborate effectively in an international team environment. Strong multitasking skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks independently. Preferred Qualifications Knowledge of power semiconductor devices, such as MOSFETs, and related characterization methods. Knowledge of PCB design or Altium Designer is desirable. Experience using MATLAB and LabVIEW. Knowledge of German is a plus.

4204d4646d93.title: Engineering Capabilities Development
4204d4646d93.description: Who We Are Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. What We Offer Salary: $163,000.00 - $224,000.00 Location: Santa Clara,CA You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits . Key Responsibilities Independently, develops and implements concepts for range of significantly complex product issues and engineering solutions Technically lead and/or execute engineering projects and spearhead research and development of new technologies, as appropriate Problem identification and troubleshooting a wide range of significantly complex engineering problems Specify or perform significantly complex engineering analysis Interface with internal and external customers regarding significantly complex engineering issues to address customers High Value Problems (HVP) for a range of products Generate product specifications. Define, coordinate, perform, and document engineering tests. Specify the development, design, or modify engineering layouts/schematics and/or detailed drawings/specifications of significantly complex scope Specify and review engineering documentation and engineering processes This technical staff position leads and/or develops and executes exceptionally complex technology and engineering projects and leads research and development of new technologies as appropriate. Works on critical complex issues that impact longer term product development and business success measured by one or more of the following: incremental revenue growth, market share increases, key customer penetrations, time to market improvement and commercial value of the work. Within safety guidelines design, perform, collect data, analyze and compile reports on unusually complex engineering experiments and provides solutions which are highly innovative and ingenious. 8a88e6fca97d.description: The position is described below. If you want to apply, click the Apply Now button at the top or bottom of this page. After you click Apply Now and complete your application, you'll be invited to create a profile, which will let you see your application status and any communications. If you already have a profile with us, you can log in to check status. Need Help? If you have a disability and need assistance with the application, you can request a reasonable accommodation. Send an email to Accessibility (accommodation requests only; other inquiries won't receive a response). Regular or Temporary: Regular Language Fluency: English (Required) Work Shift: 1st shift (United States of America) Please review the following job description: Perform UiPath RPA platform installations, configuration, and level 2 incident support for Truist’s Intelligent Automation program. Rapidly triage and resolve any escalated platform problems. Perform remediation tasks to address platform compliance issues. Consult and partner with Intelligent Automation developers to add or improve platform capabilities as needed. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Following is a summary of the essential functions for this job. Other duties may be performed, both major and minor, which are not mentioned below. Specific activities may change from time to time. Perform on-prem and SaaS cloud UiPath RPA platform installations, configuration, and level 3 incident support for Truist’s Intelligent Automation program. Lead and participate in rapid triage and resolution of escalated UiPath RPA platform and automated process problems. This often involves problem isolation skills to determine root cause and solution. Variables can include robot VDI configuration anomalies, robot VDI browser version nuances, Windows IIS server issues, Active Directory/Entra permissions, network & firewall configuration challenges, O365 feature incompatibilities, and more. Build and maintain innovative automation solutions for monitoring, metrics, program enablement, and insights. Incorporate security controls and monitoring in support of company standards. Lead moderately complex projects and participate in larger, more complex initiatives. In an Agile environment: Responsible for performing automation platform maintenance and improvement tasks. Leverage continuous engineering practices to deliver business value. Actively participate in expressing and refining user stories. Provide automated monitoring capabilities, warranty support, and knowledge transfer to production support. Implement user stories and tasks in accordance with the acceptance criteria established by the Product Owner. d2c3f90e8ba1.title: Web Designer and Developer
d2c3f90e8ba1.description: Department ESA Creative Operations and Web Design About the Department The Office of College Admissions has a staff of over 40 people, most of whom are Admissions Counselors. They manage marketing to, and recruitment of, prospective students as well as the admissions process for first year and transfer students into The College, and summer programs for high school students. The Office of College Admissions is a forward-facing department often providing the first impression of UChicago to visitors in-person and virtually, as well as telephonically and via email engagement. Of its several schools and departments, The College is the single-largest entity and this office is responsible for bringing to campus a class of students with a diverse set of backgrounds who are prepared to take on rigorous academics while engaging with the University, surrounding neighborhood, and city of Chicago. For more information please visit our website at http://www.collegeadmissions.uchicago.edu. Job Summary The University of Chicago’s Enrollment and Student Advancement Division (ESA) seeks a highly organized, creative, and detail-oriented web designer and developer to support communications and outreach across the division. This position will have a particular focus on designing web and digital offerings for student career programs, University Master’s programs, and college access initiatives like STARS (Small Town and Rural Students Network). This role is central to translating the division’s work into compelling, accessible materials for diverse audiences while ensuring consistent, high-quality execution across digital engagements. The position will collaborate closely with project leads, contribute to outreach strategy, and support the day-to-day operations that advance the division’s mission. This position reports to ESA’s Executive Director of Creative Operations and works closely with project and technical leads across affiliated departments. Responsibilities Supports web development and design for ESA websites, with a particular focus on sites dedicated to student career programs, master’s recruitment, and college access initiatives like STARS. Maintains and updates user interface and content on divisional websites to ensure accuracy, accessibility, and consistency. Works closely with colleagues in the University’s Career Advancement and College Admissions offices to scope project requirements and source content. Edits and manages visual assets, including maintaining an organized photo and digital asset library. Supports content development across platforms, ensuring alignment with institutional standards and messaging. Attends meetings with project leads and stakeholders; prepares clear, actionable notes and summaries. Assists in coordinating programming and outreach activities for divisional initiatives. Tracks project timelines and deliverables to support successful execution. Contributes to defining and refining outreach goals for ESA initiatives. Evaluate

0b2064945ddc.title: Software Engineer III - Equity Linked Technology
0b2064945ddc.description: Job Description: At Bank of America, we are guided by a common purpose to help make financial lives better through the power of every connection. We do this by driving Responsible Growth and delivering for our clients, teammates, communities and shareholders every day. Being a Great Place to Work and providing a culture of caring is core to how we drive Responsible Growth. We are intentional about fostering an inclusive workplace where every teammate has the opportunity to succeed, build a career and contribute to our shared success. This includes attracting and developing exceptional talent, recognizing and rewarding performance, and supporting our teammates’ physical, emotional, and financial wellness through affordable, competitive and flexible benefits. We value the unique perspectives individuals bring from all backgrounds and career paths - whether shaped by military service, community college education, or a wide range of work and life experiences. These journeys foster resilience, leadership and innovation, strengthening our workforce and positively impact the communities we serve. Bank of America is committed to an in-office culture that supports collaboration, engagement, and career development. Our approach includes clear in-office expectations, while providing an appropriate level of flexibility based on role-specific responsibilities and business needs. At Bank of America, you can build a successful career with opportunities to learn, grow, and make an impact. Join us! Job Description: This job is responsible for developing and delivering complex requirements to accomplish business goals. Key responsibilities of the job include ensuring that software is developed to meet functional, non-functional and compliance requirements, and solutions are well designed with maintainability/ease of integration and testing built-in from the outset. Job expectations include a strong knowledge of development and testing practices common to the industry and design and architectural patterns. Overview of Business Area The Equity Linked Technology group within Global Markets provides development and support for the Equity Derivatives business globally. The product sets include Structured Notes, with functions spanning pricing, execution, trade capture, and lifecycle processing. Team The role is part of the Product Modelling & Data Capture initiative, a key capability of the Structured Notes strategic buildout program. The team is responsible for defining a common set of heuristics, data models, and applications for structured products, serving as the central hub for the new platform buildout across origination and manufacturing workflows. Operating in a fast-paced, growing business line, the team adheres to Agile delivery methodologies with frequent incremental releases. The technology stack includes Java, Python and React , offering opportunities to work closely with front-office stakeholders and directly influence the success of the platform. Job Pro

8a0f62ea3a4e.title: Building Automation Engineer, Maintenance (Full Time)
8a0f62ea3a4e.description: Benefis is one of Montana’s largest and premier health systems, and we are committed to providing excellent care for all, healing body, mind, and spirit. At Benefis, we work hard to support our employees in every aspect of their careers by offering outstanding benefits and compensation, state-of-the-art facilities, and multiple growth opportunities. The only thing missing is you! Responsible for designing, programing, installing, and maintaining computerized systems for HVAC, lighting, and security, to optimize building performance, improve energy efficiency, and enhance occupant comfort. Troubleshoot control systems, manage network connectivity, troubleshoot/install and calibrate field devices, and ensure compliance with safety standards. Perform routine inspections and preventative maintenance. Collaborate with contractors, vendors, and facility staff to implement upgrades and new technology. Provides education and training to Facilities Engineering team members on the installation and operations of automated systems controls equipment and computerized controls functions. Demonstrates the ability to deal with pressure to meet deadlines, to be accurate, and to handle constantly changing situations. Demonstrates the ability to deal with a variety of people, deal with stressful situations, and handle conflict. Will perform all job duties or job tasks as assigned. Will follow and adhere to all requirements, regulations and procedures of any licensing board or agency. Must comply with all Benefis Health System’s organization policies and procedures Education/License/Experience Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in mechanical or electrical engineering, or equivalent technical experience. Three (3) to five (5) years of experience in building automation or HVAC controls. Relevant certifications (e.g., CEM - Certified Energy Manager, LEED) are highly valued. Experience with programming and graphics software for platforms including Johnson Controls, Honeywell, Delta and Schneider. Proficient in HVAC systems, Low Voltage electrical circuits, and building automation protocols (BACnet, Modbus, LonWorks) Exemption Wage Non-Exempt Starting Wage $32.49/hourly Actual offered wage is based on applicable experience

d3ca9e37702d.title: Associate Data Engineer I
d3ca9e37702d.description: The Associate Data Engineer I will help build, maintain, and optimize data infrastructure - like databases and data pipelines - that address cross-functional critical business needs. This role is expected to improve existing methods and processes by using various languages to pull and manipulate data, develop and maintain ETL pipelines, implement quality control and monitoring processes to maintain accurate data flows, maintain comprehensive documentation of process designs, test results, and pipeline configurations, etc. Responsibilities Design and develop data solutions to address business challenges. Demonstrate knowledge across a range of technologies related to programming languages, data integration, data warehousing, and data visualization and analysis. Responsible for building, maintaining, and optimizing data infrastructure. Ensure data is collected, processed, and stored efficiently and securely. Integrate different data sources and create the foundations on which analytics and data science can operate. Understand the broad objectives of projects as well as position's role in achieving objectives; modify approach when required. Escalates issues to ensure execution success and keeps stakeholders informed through effective verbal and written communication. Learn, understand and master new methods and technologies, and act as a resource or mentor for others. Produces and maintains required technical documentation associated with work products. Adhere to corporate standards regarding applicable Corporate and Divisional Policies, including code of conduct, safety, GxP compliance, data security, and the software development lifecycle. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science or relevant field. Programming skills in Java, Python, R, or SQL. Prior exposure to data engineering. Preferred Qualifications Experience interacting with APIs to extract data. Hands-on experience using large language models and machine learning algorithms. Demonstrated experience applying AI/ML in clinical or pharmaceutical contexts.

21130e3e33b1.description: The Associate Data Engineer II will build, maintain, and optimize data infrastructure - like databases and data pipelines - that address cross-functional critical business needs. This role is expected to improve existing methods and processes by using various languages to pull and manipulate data, develop and maintain ETL pipelines, implement quality control and monitoring processes to maintain accurate data flows, maintain comprehensive documentation of process designs, test results, and pipeline configurations, etc. This hire is expected to demonstrate proficiency in information technology engineering, including data warehousing. Responsibilities Independently design and develop data solutions to address business challenges. Demonstrate proficiency across a range of technologies related to programming languages, data integration, data warehousing, data visualization and data analysis. Responsible for building, maintaining, and optimizing data infrastructure. Ensure data is collected, processed, and stored efficiently and securely. Integrate different data sources and create the foundations on which analytics and data science can operate. Understand the broad objectives of projects as well as position's role in achieving objectives; modify approach when required. Escalates issues to ensure execution success and keeps stakeholders informed through effective verbal and written communication. Learn, understand and master new methods and technologies, and act as a resource or mentor for others. Produces and maintains required technical documentation associated with work products. Adhere to corporate standards regarding applicable Corporate and Divisional Policies, including code of conduct, safety, GxP compliance, data security, and the software development lifecycle. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s Degree - preferably in Computer Science or Data Science - plus 2 years of experience, Master’s degree with 0 years of experience. Programming skills in Java, Python, R, or SQL. Prior experience with data engineering. Preferred Qualifications Experience interacting with APIs to extract data. Hands-on experience using large language models and machine learning algorithms. Demonstrated experience applying AI/ML in clinical or pharmaceutical contexts.

092d10306b4a.title: Architecture and System Design Engineer
092d10306b4a.description: We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Principal Engineer specializing in Electronics Architecture and System Design to join our innovative team in Irvine, United States. In this pivotal role, you will lead the development of cutting-edge electronic systems, driving technological advancements and shaping the future of our products. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Must understand all system implications and provide a robust electronics solution for our products Utilize core functions for various IP that is developed while ensuring we meet performance, cost and reliability goals Must be able to understand and optimize power, thermal, mechanical, and electrical aspects Support Servo-Mechanical team with Spindle and VCM design, Load/unload power simulations, seek headroom simulations, spin up/down simulations Design, simulate, and validate power and voltage regulator systems for accuracy, stability, thermal management, and ripple control Set up and review power integrity analysis and signal integrity analysis with internal core teams and outside services Work with internal FW teams to bring up and validate new devices, understand needs and optimizations for future product plans and execute those needs with new device and PCBA designs Develop new silicon devices with core teams and materials to support our product needs such as SOC features, PLSI features, unique NAND devices, power management devices, and sensors Work with internal and core materials teams to understand component costs to make tradeoff analysis for each system to ensure best cost and performance solutions Gain information from core functions such as SoC power analysis, egress data rates, Power LSI efficiency, Spindle motor parameters to generate power, VCM parameters to control speed in power loss, Memory and NAND device specifications for Egress and SOC interface requirements Work with internal or outsourced service to capture the schematic, complete layout and produce manufacturing Gerber files and documentation Work with factory, core teams, and suppliers to create new part numbers, execute component qualification, create Bill of Materials for new assemblies, release new PCBA drawings, and complete DFM reviews for new board designs. Support customer facing internal and core teams with product specs, presentations, and rapidly resolving and customer issues Support content solutions team electronics needs for OEM products, proposal reviews, design reviews, cost analysis, and issue resolution Perform and direct lab work as needed for experiments, validation of new devices, and validation of new board designs Lead cross-functional teams in the development and implementation of electronic solutions Collaborate with hardware and software teams to ensure seamless integration of electronic components Troubleshoot complex issues and provide innovative solutions to engineering challenges Develop and maintain system-level specifications and design documentation Mentor junior
